query:
Research the company "jdepeets".

Research the company's recent financial performance and strategic signals: (1) Q-over-Q or recent quarterly earnings misses or guidance cuts, particularly large revenue gaps ($500M+); (2) evidence of production/capacity growth that hasn't translated to proportional P&L gains; (3) recent CEO transitions or leadership changes in the past 12 months; (4) pulled, reduced, or delayed 2026 guidance announcements; (5) new product platform launches or major go-to-market initiatives; (6) supply chain, production, or manufacturing capacity investments; (7) pricing strategy changes or promotional activity changes; (8) distribution expansion or market entry initiatives; (9) margin compression signals despite operational wins. Focus on structural P&L gaps where individual functions appear healthy but the consolidated business is underperforming.

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
**Executive Summary**

Over the past 12 months, JDE Peet's has exhibited severe structural margin compression, characterized by aggressive pricing actions that successfully drove top-line revenue but masked underlying volume declines and stagnant profitability. Despite significant historical investments in manufacturing capacity, unprecedented commodity inflation has prevented these operational expansions from translating into proportional P&L gains. Consequently, the company has pivoted toward footprint optimization, divesting low-margin segments and closing factories. In Q1 2026, Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) completed the acquisition of JDE Peet's, shifting the company's strategic trajectory toward post-merger integration and synergy realization.

**Financial Performance and Margin Compression**

JDE Peet's recent financial results reveal a structural disconnect between revenue growth and profitability, driven heavily by commodity inflation and pricing actions:
*   **Top-Line Growth vs. Volume Declines:** For the full year 2025, JDE Peet's reported trailing 12-month revenue of $11.2 billion [JDE Peet's 2026 Company Profile: Stock Performance & Earnings | PitchBook](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/company/55464-40). The company achieved organic sales growth of 15.3%, but this was driven entirely by a 19.5% price increase, while actual volume/mix declined by 4.3% [JDE Peet’s reports full-year results 2025](https://uk.finance.yahoo.com/news/jde-peet-reports-full-results-060000706.html). 
*   **Stagnant Profitability:** Despite the double-digit revenue growth, organic adjusted EBIT grew a mere 1.2% to €1.3 billion in FY 2025, signaling significant margin compression [JDE Peet’s reports full-year results 2025](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/jde-peet-reports-full-results-060000335.html). The company cited "unprecedented commodity inflation" and record cost inflation throughout the year [JDE Peet's Reports 12.3% Growth in 2025 Despite Record Cost Inflation](https://www.ideal-investisseur.fr/en/stock-news/jde-peets-reports-123-growth-in-2025-despite-record-cost-inflation/14341.html). 
*   **Segment-Specific Weakness:** The Peet's segment was particularly impacted. In H1 2025, the segment's adjusted EBIT plunged 37.6% organically despite a 4.1% organic sales growth, which leadership attributed to the phasing of inflation and pricing [JDE Peet’s reports half-year results 2025](https://www.jdepeets.com/siteassets/home/investors/financial-reports/jde-peets-half-year-results-2025-report.pdf).
*   **Muted Forward Guidance:** Prior to its acquisition, JDE Peet's projected that its adjusted operating profit would grow by only 3% to 4% in 2026-2027, lagging behind an expected gross profit increase of 4% to 7% [JDE Peet’s unveils ‘Reignite the Amazing’ strategy to drive sustainable growth, brand simplification](https://www.foodbusinessmea.com/jde-peets-unveils-reignite-the-amazing-strategy-to-drive-sustainable-growth-brand-simplification/).

**Supply Chain Investments and Capacity Optimization**

JDE Peet's has aggressively invested in production capacity, but recent actions indicate a need to rationalize its footprint as capacity growth failed to deliver proportional earnings:
*   **Capacity Expansion:** The company previously invested €110 million to increase aluminum coffee capsule production capacity by more than 60% [JDE Peet’s to significantly expand its aluminium coffee capsule production capacity](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2020/10/20/2110765/0/en/JDE-Peet-s-to-significantly-expand-its-aluminium-coffee-capsule-production-capacity.html) and acquired the Brazilian coffee and tea business Maratá for €682 million in January 2024, which included two manufacturing plants [JDE Peet’s reports full-year results 2024](https://www.jdepeets.com/siteassets/home/investors/financial-reports/jde-peets-full-year-results-2024-report.pdf).
*   **Footprint Rationalization:** To correct structural inefficiencies, JDE Peet's closed three factories in 2025 located in Banbury (U.K.), northeast Brazil, and the northeast U.S. [JDE Peet’s reports strategic and operational progress - Confirms 2025 outlook](https://www.jdepeets.com/news-container/jde-peets-reports-strategic-and-operational-progress---confirms-2025-outlook-3174303/). 
*   **Productivity Initiatives:** Under its "Reignite the Amazing" strategy, the company is targeting €500 million in net productivity savings by the end of 2027 [JDE Peet’s reveals growth strategy](https://www.gcrmag.com/jde-peets-reveals-growth-strategy/). It is also rolling out the IRIS (Intelligent, Responsive, and Integrated Supply Chain Planning) system globally to manufacturing units through 2026 [JDE Peet's Goes Live with OMP's Unison Planning(TM), Accelerating Supply Chain Value at Scale](https://www.accessnewswire.com/newsroom/en/computers-technology-and-internet/jde-peets-goes-live-with-omps-unison-planningtm-accelerating-supp-1150074).

**Go-to-Market Shifts and Product Launches**

In late 2025 and early 2026, JDE Peet's executed several major go-to-market shifts to optimize resource allocation and expand its global footprint:
*   **U.S. Strategy Shift:** In 2025, the company discontinued the rollout of its L’Or Barista machines in the United States and transferred the L’OR capsules business to the Peet’s brand to optimize resource allocation [JDE Peet’s reports strategic and operational progress - Confirms 2025 outlook [3174303] | JDE Peet's](https://www.jdepeets.com/news-container/jde-peets-reports-strategic-and-operational-progress---confirms-2025-outlook-3174303/).
*   **Divestitures:** The company divested its Turkish tea business, which generated €60 million in annual sales but had a negative adjusted EBIT contribution, as well as its low-margin Food Ingredients (B2B) business in Asia [JDE Peet's reports Q1 2025 results - Global Coffee Report](https://www.gcrmag.com/jde-peets-reports-q1-2025-results/).
*   **Market Expansion & Licensing:** JDE Peet's is executing an aggressive rollout of Peet’s Coffee in China, targeting 400 store locations by the end of 2026 [What is Growth Strategy and Future Prospects of JDE Peet's Company? – MatrixBCG.com](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/growth-strategy/jdepeets). Additionally, in early 2026, it completed a long-term global license agreement to manufacture and sell Caribou-branded coffee products for €246 million [Newsroom | JDE Peet's](https://www.jdepeets.com/media/news).
*   **New Platforms:** On June 8, 2026, the company launched a new global marketing platform, “Official Sponsor of Staying Awake,” positioning its coffee for late-night and early-morning football viewing [JDE Peet’s Unveils “Official Sponsor of Staying Awake”: a new global platform, turning late-night football fandom into the ultimate new coffee occasion](https://retailtimes.co.uk/jde-peets-unveils-official-sponsor-of-staying-awake-a-new-global-platform-turning-late-night-football-fandom-into-the-ultimate-new-coffee-occasion/). In early 2026, it also opened an €8 million Innovation Laboratory in Utrecht to accelerate product development [JDE Peet’s New Utrecht Lab Set To Transform Coffee Market | ESM Magazine](https://www.esmmagazine.com/a-brands/jde-peets-new-utrecht-lab-set-to-transform-coffee-market-297986).

**Corporate Control: Keurig Dr Pepper Acquisition**

The most significant strategic event of 2026 was the acquisition of JDE Peet's by Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP):
*   On January 16, 2026, KDP subsidiary Kodiak BidCo B.V. launched a recommended public cash offer for all outstanding shares of JDE Peet's at €31.85 per share [JDE Peet’s reports full-year results 2025](https://uk.finance.yahoo.com/news/jde-peet-reports-full-results-060000706.html).
*   The acquisition was completed on March 27, 2026, with a settlement date of April 1, 2026, after 96.22% of shares were tendered [Press Releases | JDE Peet's](https://www.jdepeets.com/investors/news--events/press-releases/).
*   KDP expects the integration of JDE Peet's to deliver $400 million in synergies and contribute 6-7 percentage points to earnings growth [KDP Q1 Deep Dive: Transformation Efforts, JDE Peet's Integration, and Segment Divergence](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/kdp-q1-deep-dive-transformation-204523798.html).

**Gaps and Limitations**

Based on the provided findings, the following requested data points could not be verified:
*   Evidence of recent CEO transitions or executive leadership changes within the past 12 months.
*   Specific instances of large revenue gaps or misses exceeding $500 million in recent quarters.
*   Announcements regarding pulled, reduced, or delayed 2026 guidance (findings only indicate muted 3-4% EBIT growth guidance for 2026-2027 prior to the KDP acquisition).
